items.title: Stocks Broke the Wall. Oil Didn't Get the Memo.
items.copy:
The S&P closed back above 7,504 on a memory-chip rally — and Brent closed above Monday's high on the same day. One of those reads on the war is wrong.

Post-close read of Tuesday, July 21 on settled prints: Micron's 12% day and the South Korea export data behind it, Nvidia's 9.3% Nebius stake, the Hormuz tanker hit and the Houthi blockade threat, Brent through 91.42, XLE confirming the premium, the 10-year touching its July high, the VIX refund, and Danaher's guide-cut lesson for earnings season. Charts are TradingView settled daily bars; sources: Reuters via BNN Bloomberg, The Motley Fool, Yahoo Finance, Benzinga, PR Newswire, Investing.com, and the Federal Reserve calendar.

Three cases off the close, each with its invalidation, ahead of Alphabet and Tesla on Wednesday. The two lines that decide the week: Brent 91.42 and the 10-year yield at 4.64.

Research tooling, not financial advice. No performance is promised or implied.
